title: Waypoint Progress Spine
prompt:
Create a production-ready Waypoint Progress Spine using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original embedded reading effect represents a three-part article as a vertical spine with named waypoints. A reader can select Context, Evidence, or Decision using accessible controls; the highlighted waypoint moves and the matching short passage becomes visible. It must work as a compact card preview and as a larger detail surface without relying on the page scroll position. Use a light Bento system with #F4F5F6 stage, white panel, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, soft neutral shadow, #111111 type, and #4A4A4F secondary copy. Lime is the only bright accent and the card itself must use solid fills.

Use native buttons with aria-pressed, a labelled group, and an aria-live polite progress sentence. Click, Enter, and Space select each waypoint. Move the lime waypoint with a spring at stiffness 320 and damping 27; fade the passage in over 150ms ease-out. Keep state local and deterministic; no scroll listeners, timers, random values, storage, remote data, network calls, or browser-global access. Use transform and opacity for all animation.

At 320px, stack controls and passage cleanly; at larger sizes, preserve a readable line length. Maintain logical tab order, a visible 3px focus ring, AA contrast, and textual location feedback beyond the lime indicator. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by moving the marker immediately and retaining a brief opacity fade. Return complete client code, CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML: <section class="waypoint-spine"><button aria-pressed="true">1. Context</button></section> */
.waypoint-spine{max-width:340px;padding:20px;border-left:8px solid #f4f5f6;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.waypoint-spine button{display:block;min-height:44px;width:100%;margin:6px 0;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:12px;background:#fff;color:#111;font-weight:800;text-align:left}.waypoint-spine button[aria-pressed=true]{border-left:5px solid #d8f34e}.waypoint-spine button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.waypoint-spine *{transition:none}}

demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [a,setA]=React.useState(0);const m=[['Context','Name the reader’s starting point.'],['Evidence','Show the clearest supporting signal.'],['Decision','Record the reversible next step.']];return
READING WAYPOINT{m[a][0]}

{m[a][1]}

}return ;})()
